description_long: This object consists of two black karambit knives, each featuring a dramatically curved, single-edged blade with a sharp point. The blades are matte black and appear to be made of metal, with a smooth, slightly reflective finish. The handles are ergonomically contoured with finger grooves and a textured grip, designed for secure handling, and each handle ends in a prominent ring for improved retention and control. The spine of each handle has a series of ridges, likely for thumb placement or added grip. There are no visible decorative elements or engravings, giving the knives a tactical, utilitarian appearance. The overall design is compact and aggressive, suitable for close-quarters use.
description: A pair of black, curved karambit knives with ergonomic, textured handles and ringed pommels, featuring matte metal blades and a tactical design.
description_view_0: Two black karambit knives are shown side by side, highlighting their curved blades, finger-grooved handles, and ringed pommels.
description_view_1: The knives are viewed edge-on, emphasizing the thinness of the blades and the alignment of the handles and rings.
description_view_2: The opposite side of the knives is visible, showing the full curvature of the blades and the textured handles with finger grooves.
description_view_3: A narrow, top-down view reveals the slim profile of the blades and the alignment of the handles and rings.
attribute_handle_design: ergonomic, contoured polymer handle with finger grooves, textured grip, and ringed pommel
